questions.text: A quantity that has both magnitude and direction is called a:
questions.explanation: Vectors require both specifications to be fully defined.

questions.text: The magnitude of a vector a = xi + yj + zk is given by:
questions.explanation: This is the distance formula from the origin in 3D space.

questions.text: A vector whose magnitude is unity is called a:
questions.explanation: Unit vectors are used to specify direction; denoted by a cap (â).

questions.text: Vectors having the same initial point are called:
questions.explanation: By definition, co-initial vectors start from the same point.

questions.text: The position vector of a point P(x, y, z) is:
questions.explanation: It represents the vector from the origin O(0,0,0) to point P.

questions.text: If a and b are two collinear vectors, then which of the following is true?
options: b = λa for some scalar λ
questions.explanation: Collinearity implies they are parallel, so one is a scalar multiple of the other.

questions.text: The unit vector in the direction of vector a is:
questions.explanation: Dividing a vector by its magnitude results in a vector of length 1 in the same direction.

questions.text: The direction cosines l, m, n of a vector satisfy the relation:
questions.explanation: The sum of squares of direction cosines is always unity.

questions.text: The scalar product (dot product) of two vectors a and b is:
questions.explanation: a . b = |a||b| cos θ, where θ is the angle between them.

questions.text: If the dot product of two non-zero vectors is zero, then the vectors are:
questions.explanation: a . b = 0 implies cos θ = 0, so θ = 90°.

questions.text: The value of i . i is:
questions.explanation: Dot product of a unit vector with itself is 1 (cos 0 = 1).

questions.text: The value of i . j is:
questions.explanation: i and j are orthogonal (perpendicular), so their dot product is 0.

questions.text: The projection of vector a on vector b is given by:
questions.explanation: It is the scalar component of a in the direction of b.

questions.text: The vector product (cross product) of two vectors a and b is:
questions.explanation: The result of a cross product is a vector perpendicular to both a and b.

questions.text: The magnitude of a x b represents the ________ of a parallelogram with sides a and b.
questions.explanation: Area = |a x b| = |a||b| sin θ.

questions.text: The value of i x i is:
questions.explanation: Cross product of a vector with itself is the zero vector (sin 0 = 0).
The cross product of any vector with itself is the zero vector.

questions.text: According to the right-hand screw rule, i x j equals:
questions.explanation: In a right-handed system, the cross product of i and j is k.

questions.text: The area of a triangle with adjacent sides as vectors a and b is:
questions.explanation: It is half the area of the corresponding parallelogram.

questions.text: If a = 2i + j + 3k and b = 3i + 5j - 2k, then a . b is:
Two vectors are equal if they have:
questions.explanation: Equality of vectors is independent of their position in space (unless they are localized vectors).

questions.text: Direction ratios of a vector xi + yj + zk are:
questions.explanation: The components x, y, z serve as direction ratios.

questions.text: If |a| = 2, |b| = 5 and |a x b| = 8, then a . b is:
Which property does the cross product NOT follow?
questions.explanation: a x b = -(b x a), so it is anti-commutative.

questions.text: The vector joining points P(x1, y1, z1) and Q(x2, y2, z2) is:
questions.explanation: Vector PQ = Position vector of Q - Position vector of P.

questions.text: Scalar triple product [a b c] represents the ________ of a parallelepiped.
questions.explanation: [a b c] = a . (b x c) gives the volume.

questions.text: If [a b c] = 0, then the vectors a, b, c are:
questions.explanation: Zero volume implies the vectors lie in the same plane.

questions.text: The angle between two vectors a and b can be found using:
questions.explanation: Both dot and cross product definitions can be rearranged to find the angle.

questions.text: For any vector a, a . a is equal to:
questions.explanation: The dot product of a vector with itself is the square of its magnitude.

questions.text: The section formula for internal division of PQ by point R in ratio m:n is:
questions.explanation: Standard formula where a and b are position vectors of P and Q.

questions.text: Find the unit vector in direction of 3i + 4j:
questions.explanation: Magnitude = √(3² + 4²) = 5. Unit vector = (3/5)i + (4/5)j.

questions.text: If a is a non-zero vector of magnitude 'a' and λ is a non-zero scalar, then λa is a unit vector if:
The value of i . (j x k) + j . (i x k) + k . (i x j) is:
If θ is the angle between two vectors a and b, then |a.b| = |axb| when θ is:
questions.explanation: cos θ = sin θ => tan θ = 1 => θ = 45° or π/4.

questions.text: The direction cosines of y-axis are:
questions.explanation: y-axis makes 90, 0, 90 degrees with x, y, z axes respectively.

questions.text: Work done by a force F in displacing a body through d is:
questions.explanation: Work is a scalar quantity defined as the dot product of force and displacement.

questions.text: Which of the following is a unit vector?
The value of λ for which vectors 2i - j + k and i + 2j + λk are perpendicular is:
The position vector of the midpoint of PQ where P(2,3,4) and Q(4,1,-2) is:
If a and b are two unit vectors and θ is the angle between them, then a+b is a unit vector if θ is:
The projection of i on j is:
questions.explanation: i and j are perpendicular, so the component of one along the other is zero.
